retry_reason: The put operation always executes; if the refcount reaches zero the object is freed; retrying would cause a use-after-free.
contract_summary: Deferred put wrapper for BPF programs. Called from a workqueue when the last external reference is released (via bpf_prog_put). It performs final cleanup: triggers a perf event for program unload, an audit event, frees the program's ID, and then decrements the core reference counts (module, BTF, attach BTF) via __bpf_prog_put_noref. This function is the final release path; after it runs, the program object may be freed. It does not return a reference or transfer ownership — it consumes the reference that was held by the work item. The caller must not use the program after scheduling this work.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to release a BPF program's references (put). It calls __bpf_prog_put_noref to decrement refcounts, but also includes non-refcount cleanup steps (perf event, audit, freeing ID). Thus it is a put wrapper with some associated logistics.
judge.retry_reason: The put operation always executes; if the refcount reaches zero the object is freed; retrying would cause a use-after-free.

check.reason: All three operations are consistent: the callee __bpf_prog_put_noref acts as a put on its first parameter prog, which is derived from the caller's parameter 1 (work) via container_of and aux->prog. The access paths trace from work through _container (representing container_of) to aux, then to prog, then to the respective refcount fields (mod.refcnt, btf.refcnt.refs, attach_btf.refcnt.refs). The segment counts and type chains match. The direction 'put' is correct as per callee info.
